questions.question: Why are goods in weekly markets usually cheaper than in shopping malls?

options.A: Sellers do not pay high rent

questions.explanation: Weekly market traders do not have to pay expensive shop rent, which helps keep prices lower.

questions.question: Which factor allows neighbourhood shops to maintain strong relationships with customers?
questions.explanation: Neighbourhood shops often allow customers to buy on credit, building trust and long-term relationships.

questions.question: What role do wholesalers play in the market chain?

options.B: Buy goods in bulk and supply retailers

questions.explanation: Wholesalers purchase goods in large quantities from producers and sell them to retailers.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of weekly markets for sellers?

options.B: They can sell goods at different places on different days
options.D: They operate only once a year

questions.explanation: Weekly market traders can move from one market to another throughout the week.

questions.question: Why do shopping malls generally sell goods at higher prices?
questions.explanation: Malls have higher rent, electricity, staff salaries, and maintenance costs.

questions.question: Which type of seller usually buys goods from wholesalers?
questions.explanation: Retailers purchase goods from wholesalers and sell them to consumers.

questions.question: What is one major benefit of neighbourhood markets for consumers?

options.B: They are located close to homes

questions.explanation: Neighbourhood markets are convenient because they are easily accessible.

questions.question: Which step usually comes first in the chain of markets?
questions.explanation: The chain begins with producers who manufacture or grow goods.

questions.question: Why do people prefer weekly markets for fresh produce?

options.B: Fresh vegetables and fruits are available

questions.explanation: Weekly markets often sell fresh produce brought directly by farmers or traders.

questions.question: Which type of market usually has fixed prices and branded products?
questions.explanation: Shopping malls sell branded products with fixed prices.

questions.question: Which factor helps neighbourhood shops compete with large malls?
questions.explanation: Neighbourhood shopkeepers often know their customers personally and provide flexible services.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a market?
questions.explanation: Markets allow buyers and sellers to exchange goods and services.

questions.question: Which market type provides the widest range of products in one place?
questions.explanation: Shopping malls contain many stores offering a large variety of products.

questions.question: Why do retailers buy goods in bulk from wholesalers?

options.A: To reduce costs and ensure steady supply

questions.explanation: Buying in bulk lowers the price per unit and ensures availability of goods.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a neighbourhood market?

options.B: Small local shops serving nearby residents

questions.explanation: Neighbourhood markets consist of small shops that cater to local residents.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of weekly markets?

options.A: They operate only once a week

questions.explanation: Since weekly markets operate only once a week, they may not always be convenient.

questions.question: Why are malls popular among some consumers?
questions.explanation: Malls offer air-conditioning, entertainment, and organized shopping spaces.

questions.question: What is the main link between producers and consumers in the market chain?
questions.explanation: Retailers and wholesalers help move goods from producers to consumers.

questions.question: Which feature distinguishes malls from weekly markets?
questions.explanation: Malls have permanent shops, unlike temporary stalls in weekly markets.

questions.question: Why do many small traders prefer weekly markets?
questions.explanation: Operating in weekly markets requires less investment and lower costs.

questions.question: What type of goods are usually found in neighbourhood shops?
questions.explanation: Neighbourhood shops mainly sell daily necessities like groceries and toiletries.

questions.question: Which group benefits most from the availability of markets?
questions.explanation: Markets help producers sell goods and consumers obtain what they need.

questions.question: Which stage in the market chain sells goods directly to consumers?
questions.explanation: Retailers interact directly with consumers.

questions.question: Which factor influences where sellers choose to set up shops?
questions.explanation: Sellers prefer locations with more customers to increase sales.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the chain of markets?

options.A: To connect producers to consumers efficiently

questions.explanation: The chain of markets ensures goods move smoothly from producers to consumers.
